title: osmanthus aquifolium var. myrtifolius
intro: Osmanthus aquifolium var. myrtifolius, also known as the tea olive, is a plant species of the osmanthus genus that is native to China and is known for its distinctive myrtle-like foliage. The plant is used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Osmanthus aquifolium var. myrtifolius include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared as a tea or used in herbal infusions, where its essential oils are extracted to create topical treatments for skin conditions and wounds.
